Promise.line: No vendor affiliations. No production company kickbacks. No agency parent. Just senior production expertise on your side.

Independence.headline: Most production budgets are negotiated by people paid by the outcome. We are not.
Independence.body_p1: We do not own a production company. We are not part of an agency holding group.
Independence.body_p2: That is the entire premise. Everything else (the controlling, the advisory, the org design, the AI) works because the foundation is independent.

Closing.title: Tell us what you are trying to figure out.
Closing.body: A 45 minute call. We will come back within 24 hours with an honest read on whether we are the right people to help, and which founder you would be working with.

Meta.title: About — shift.media
Meta.description: shift.media exists to give brands an independent voice in production, built on expertise, not affiliation.

Hero.title: Built on expertise. Not affiliation.
Hero.subtitle: shift.media exists to give brands an independent voice in production, without the conflicts of interest built into the brand, agency, production system.

Triangle.title: The brand, agency, production triangle has built in conflicts.
Triangle.intro: Three roles. Three relationships. Zero independent oversight. Every seat at the table is paid by the others, which means no one is paid to push back.

nodes.description: Pays the bill. Wants outcomes, transparency, and value for money. Not bundled invoices and unread line items.
nodes.quote: Are we paying for the idea, or the markup on the people making it?

nodes.description: Sells the idea. Compensation is often a percentage of the production budget it specs. Bigger spec, bigger fee.
nodes.quote: Whose margin lives inside whose line item, and who is going to flag it?

nodes.description: Makes the work. Earns through markups, kit, and crew. Every cost out is a cut to its own P&L. Cannot grade its own homework.
nodes.quote: Who tells me when this bid is fair, when no one in the room is paid to say so?

Triangle.caption: Three roles · three relationships · zero independent oversight
Triangle.outro: A market this complex, this unregulated, and this large does not need another agency. It needs an independent partner.

items.title: Genuinely independent
items.description: We are not a production company. We are not an agency. We have no financial relationships with the directors or production companies we recommend. Our advice is only ever in the client's interest.

items.title: Real production knowledge
items.description: Our team has built, run, and controlled productions. We have been on set, in the edit suite, and in the negotiations. Every recommendation is grounded in what actually happens. Not what a slide deck says should happen.

items.title: Methodology, not opinions
items.description: shift.media operates on a documented methodology (the Shift Method), proprietary tooling, and a replicable advisory framework. Every engagement runs on the same diagnostic, the same playbooks, and the same review structure. Clients get institutional quality. Not individual availability.

items.title: Built for where it's going
items.description: Most production advisors were built for the industry as it was. We were built for the one that's coming. AI native operating models, in housing, budget accountability, and the unbundling of the agency model. We started there. We didn't bolt it on.

items.claim: AI does not replace production craft.
items.reality: It removes the parts that were never craft.

items.claim: In house is not cheaper by default.
items.reality: Done badly, it's more expensive than what it replaced.

items.claim: The best production decision is often a smaller one,

Origin.title: Why we built this.
Origin.body_p1: We started shift.media in Hamburg because the independent production advisor we'd want to hire didn't exist in Europe, at least not the way we thought it should.
Origin.body_p2: Three founders. Decades on set. One firm built around the conviction that brands deserve a partner who is genuinely on their side.
Origin.signature: Justin Stiebel · Cornelius Roenz · Jankel Huppertz

Closing.title: Forty five minutes. No sales pitch.
Closing.body: Tell us what you are trying to figure out. We will come back within 24 hours, Monday to Friday, with an honest read on whether we are the right people to help, and which founder you would be working with.

Meta.title: Services — shift.media
Meta.description: How we work with brands. Independent advisory across the full production lifecycle.

Hero.title: From a single brief to a full rebuild.
Hero.body_p1: We meet brands wherever they are. One project. A focused workshop. An embedded retainer. A complete in house production transformation.
Hero.body_p2: The architecture is the same. The scope adjusts to the engagement.

items.title: Audit
items.description: A clear, independent read of where you stand. We assess your production setup (costs, vendors, structure, process) and surface the gaps and opportunities.

items.title: Workshop
items.description: Deep, focused sessions on a single topic. AI readiness, production strategy, organisational design, or anything else. Practical and built around your real briefs.

items.title: Pilot
items.description: A defined project, start to finish. We embed alongside your team, run it independently, and leave you with a working blueprint.

items.title: Build
items.description: Advisory led design of your in house production model. Structure, talent, process, tooling. Designed and stood up alongside your team.

items.title: Engine
items.description: Senior advisory on retainer. Always on counsel across content, campaigns, and operations. Independent of any single project. Named partner ownership.

items.title: Campaign
items.description: Embedded production oversight, brief to delivery. Cost control, vendor selection, quality assurance. We sit on the brand side of the table.

items.question: How is shift.media different from a production company?
items.answer: We don't make productions. We advise on them. We have no financial interest in any production going ahead, going bigger, or going to any particular vendor.

items.question: How is shift.media different from a consultancy?
items.answer: We've actually run productions. On set. In the edit suite. In the negotiations. Our recommendations are grounded in what happens in production. Not what a slide deck says should happen.

items.question: Do you take a cut of vendor fees?
items.answer: No. Never. Our fees come from our clients. That's it.

items.question: What does an engagement typically cost?
items.answer: We scope each engagement individually. Talk to us. We'll tell you what's involved before you commit to anything.

items.question: Can you work alongside our existing agency?
items.answer: Yes. We work with brands whose agencies are managing the production, brands working directly with production companies, and brands building their own in house function. Independent oversight is the constant.

Closing.title: Forty five minutes. No sales pitch.
Closing.body: Tell us what you are trying to figure out. We will tell you in writing within five working days whether we are the right people to help, and which founder you would be working with.

Meta.title: The Shift Method — shift.media
Meta.description: A diagnostic framework for brand content operations. Six dimensions. One conversation.

Hero.title: The Shift Method.
Hero.subtitle: A diagnostic framework for brand content operations. Six dimensions. One clear roadmap.
Hero.claim_p1: Most production problems are not creative problems.
Hero.intro: The Shift Method is how we diagnose them. Across six dimensions, ending in a clear roadmap and a defensible plan.
Hero.label: Audit

Dimensions.title: Six dimensions.
Dimensions.intro: Each one a diagnostic lens. Select any dimension to explore what we look at and why.

items.title: Workflow and Process Efficiency
items.description: We map your end to end flow from brief to delivery, identify where time and money are being lost, and quantify waiting time, handoff failures, and process variance. Most production waste is invisible until someone maps it.

items.title: Technology and Systems Integration
items.description: We assess your tech stack, integration health, adoption rates, and whether your technology investment is actually serving your production goals, or creating new complexity.

items.title: Budget and Resource Allocation
items.description: We examine budget structure, cost variance patterns, financial governance, and how resources are modelled and deployed across the production lifecycle.

items.title: Team Structure and Capability
items.description: We map roles, decision rights, capability coverage, key person risk, and the architecture of progression and retention. Who does what, who decides what, and what happens when someone leaves.

items.title: Content Pipeline and Output Quality
items.description: We trace the path of content from brief through production to archive, evaluate quality control mechanisms, rework rates, and feedback loops. Where does quality get created, and where does it get eroded?

items.title: Vendor and Partner Management
items.description: We segment your vendor portfolio, assess contract structure and service levels, and evaluate the relationships that matter most to your output. Not all vendors are equal, and most brands treat them as if they are.

items.title: The Diagnostic
items.description: A RAG scored report across all six dimensions, with evidence, root cause analysis, and indicative benchmarks.

items.title: The Roadmap
items.description: A prioritised action plan. What to fix first, what to fix next, and what to leave alone.

items.title: The Recommendation
items.description: A clear view on which engagement type comes next, or whether you don't need one yet.

Closing.title: Start with a conversation.
Closing.body: The Shift Method begins with a conversation, not a sales pitch. We'll tell you whether this is the right next step, and if it isn't, what is.

Method.ProductSlideSubhead: After the Audit, where it makes sense.

Meta.title: Team — shift.media
Meta.description: The team behind shift.media. Three co founders and an independent partner network.

Hero.title: Built by people who have done this work.
Hero.subtitle: Three co founders. One independent partner network. Decades of hands on production experience, from cost control to creative delivery.

items.name: Justin Stiebel
items.p1: Production controlling and advisory specialist with deep market knowledge across German and European TVC, content, and campaign production.
items.p2: Has built and managed production budgets across formats, from small social campaigns to large scale international shoots. Knows what things cost, what gets padded, and where clients are being underserved.
items.p3: Specialist in the German market. Its rates, its production companies, its directors, and its dynamics.

items.name: Cornelius Roenz
items.discipline: Strategy, organisational design and executive production
items.p1: Production leader with extensive experience building and running production functions at agency and brand level across Europe.
items.p2: Has operated at every level of high end commercial production, from the set to the boardroom. Knows what it takes to deliver creatively demanding, commercially complex campaigns, and what it takes to lead the organisations that produce them through growth, transition, and change.
items.p3: Specialist in production strategy, organisational transformation, and sustainable structures for brands and agencies navigating a shifting market.

items.name: Jankel Huppertz
items.p1: Production leader with deep experience across agency production. Understands how TVC, content, and campaign production works, from idea to execution.
items.p2: Has built and managed production across formats and scales, from agile content to large international campaigns. Knows how to run complex productions, align teams, and deliver against creative and commercial demands.
items.p3: Specialist in production structures, processes, and budgets, and where value is created or lost.

Partners.intro: Our partner network is a curated group of senior specialists we engage on specific projects. They are independent (not staff, not employees) and they extend our expertise where it's needed.

items.title: Finance and Treasury
items.description: Independent financial oversight, treasury structures, and procurement strategy for production heavy organisations.

items.title: Strategy and Brand
items.description: Senior strategic counsel on brand positioning, content strategy, and how production serves the wider marketing system.

items.title: AI and Technology
items.description: Hands on practitioners across generative AI, production tooling, and the integration of technology into creative workflows.

items.title: Creative Direction
items.description: Award winning creative leadership for projects requiring senior creative oversight from concept through delivery.

items.title: Sustainable Production
items.description: Specialists in carbon budgeting, sustainable supplier selection, and ESG reporting across film and content production.

items.title: Legal and Rights
items.description: Music licensing, talent buyouts, IP frameworks, and the legal architecture behind compliant, modern production.

items.title: Post Production and VFX
items.description: Senior post supervisors and VFX leads who advise on pipeline design, vendor evaluation, and quality control.

items.title: International Production
items.description: Specialists in cross border production structures, offshore execution, and multi market campaign delivery.

Details.promise_value: No pitch deck. No agenda. Just an honest conversation about whether we can help, and how.

Meta.title: Workshops — shift.media
Meta.description: Four kinds of Workshops. Fixed-fee, time-boxed, ending with a written deliverable. The front door to working with SHIFT.

Hero.title: Workshops.
Hero.body: The front door to working with SHIFT. Fixed-fee, time-boxed, and ended with a written deliverable. The Workshop is where we meet your team, work alongside them, and produce something defensible together.
Hero.subline: Four kinds of Workshops. One leads to where you need to go.

Strategy.name: Strategy Workshop
Strategy.tagline: Set direction. Define what you are trying to do, and how.
Strategy.description: A Strategy Workshop runs over two days with your leadership and operational team in the room. It is structured, evidence based, and ends with a written strategic position and a twelve month roadmap. We work alongside your team to define direction, pressure test assumptions, and produce a position you can act on.
Strategy.followup: A clear position usually demands building. After a Strategy Workshop, most brands move into a multi month engagement to build out the operating model, team, or AI workflow the strategy calls for.

workshops.name: AI Readiness
workshops.hook: Map where AI runs in your production. Before it maps you.
workshops.why: Your team is using AI tools across production but no one has mapped where, on what data, with what governance. You need to know what is safe to scale and where the risk lives.
workshops.what: A two day diagnostic across six AI maturity dimensions. Tool inventory, data readiness, process suitability, governance, team capability, strategic alignment. RAG scored with your team in the room.
workshops.outcome: A written AI Readiness Report with dimension level findings, a governance MVP, and a 30/60/90 roadmap your leadership can sign off and execute.

workshops.name: AI Integration
workshops.hook: A plan to integrate AI into production without breaking quality.
workshops.why: You know where the AI gaps are. Now you need a deliberate plan to integrate AI into production workflows without breaking quality or governance.
workshops.what: A two day session covering tool selection criteria, workflow architecture, training plan, and rollout sequencing. We define which workflows are AI suitable and which are not.
workshops.outcome: A written AI Integration Plan with prioritised use cases, tool recommendations, workflow designs, and a phased rollout schedule.

workshops.name: Production Strategy
workshops.hook: Twelve months. Commissioning, costs, capacity, capability.
workshops.why: Your production activity has grown reactively, project by project. You need a deliberate twelve month strategy that aligns commissioning, costs, capacity, and capability with where the brand is going.
workshops.what: A two day strategic working session. Production volume and format analysis. Commissioning model design. Roster and capability strategy. Budget framework. Quarterly milestones.
workshops.outcome: A written Production Strategy with twelve month roadmap, quarterly milestones, and the commissioning framework to run against.

workshops.name: Operating Model Design
workshops.hook: The structure decisions you make now define capability for years.
workshops.why: You are building or restructuring your in house production. The decisions you make now about structure, decision rights, workflow, and technology will define your capability for years.
workshops.what: A two day design session. Org structure and role architecture. Decision rights map. Brief to delivery workflow. Technology stack selection. Vendor and partner principles.
workshops.outcome: A written Operating Model Document covering structure, roles, workflow, technology, and partner principles, ready to brief into the next phase of work.
workshops.cta: Book your Operating Model Design Workshop

workshops.name: Process Improvement
workshops.hook: Fix the workflow that is repeatedly slow, expensive, or broken.
workshops.why: A specific production process is repeatedly slow, expensive, or error prone. You want to fix it once, properly, with the team that runs it.
workshops.what: A two day Lean and Value Stream Mapping session applied to your highest volume or highest pain workflow. We identify waste, redesign handoffs, and define new exit criteria.
workshops.outcome: A redesigned workflow with documented exit criteria, handoff protocols, and a 30 day implementation plan.

workshops.name: The First 90 Days
workshops.hook: A deliberate plan for the new role. Not an inherited one.
workshops.why: You have just stepped into a new role as Head of Content or Head of Production. The first 90 days set the trajectory for everything that follows. You want a deliberate plan, not an inherited one.
workshops.what: A two day intensive built for the new leader. Audit of inherited operations, mapping of stakeholders and political terrain, definition of the first 90 day agenda, identification of early wins and necessary deferrals.
workshops.outcome: A written 90 day plan with stakeholder map, decision agenda, and prioritised initiatives, ready for the leader to execute from week one.

workshops.name: Day-One Studio
workshops.hook: Production done right from the start. Not retrofitted.
workshops.why: You are building a brand from scratch and want production to be done right from the start. Not retrofitted after the fact.
workshops.what: A two day greenfield design session. Brand to production blueprint. Studio structure and team design. Tool stack from day one. Vendor partner framework. Governance baked in.
workshops.outcome: A written Day-One Studio Blueprint covering structure, team, tools, partners, and governance, ready to execute from week one.

Diagnostic.name: Diagnostic Workshop
Diagnostic.tagline: Look at your own production, together. Honestly.
Diagnostic.description: A Diagnostic Workshop is one day plus pre work. The team brings the artefacts (briefs, budgets, tool lists, rosters, production records) and we run the diagnostic alongside them, not on them. The findings belong to your team because your team built them. Every diagnostic uses SHIFT's structured framework and ends with a RAG scored report.
Diagnostic.followup: A clear picture often surfaces a specific production or upcoming project that now needs hands on, independent oversight. Many brands move from a Diagnostic into an embedded engagement on that production.

workshops.name: Markup Diagnostic
workshops.hook: Find the markup you cannot see in your production budgets.
workshops.why: You suspect production costs include markup you cannot see. You want to know how much, where, and what the transparency gaps are.
workshops.what: A one day forensic against your last three to five production budgets. We work line by line with your team, compare against market benchmarks and disclosed structures, and identify markup patterns and transparency gaps.
workshops.outcome: A written Markup Diagnostic Report with line level findings, market benchmark comparison, and recommendations for transparency clauses in future briefs and contracts.

workshops.name: Roster Diagnostic
workshops.hook: An independent view of who is right for the work, and who is not.
workshops.why: Your director and production company roster has grown organically, often through agency recommendation. You want an independent view of who is right for the work, who is not, and where the gaps are.
workshops.what: A one day session reviewing your current roster against your production needs. Independent assessment of each partner on creative fit, cost, capacity, and delivery record. No financial relationships to protect.
workshops.outcome: A written Roster Diagnostic Report with partner by partner assessment, identified gaps, and a recommended roster reset.

workshops.name: Shadow AI Diagnostic
workshops.hook: A full picture of AI in use before the next legal question.
workshops.why: Your team is using AI tools you have not approved, on data you have not classified, with disclosure you have not defined. You need a complete picture before the next legal, brand, or regulatory question.
workshops.what: A one day forensic on what AI is actually in use across the production function. Tool inventory, data flow mapping, rights and disclosure review, risk register build.
workshops.outcome: A written Shadow AI Register and risk picture suitable for board level conversation, plus a 30 day governance MVP recommendation.

workshops.name: Brief Diagnostic
workshops.hook: Most production failures start in the brief.
workshops.why: Most production failures start in the brief. You suspect your briefs are inconsistent, ambiguous, or missing the inputs production teams need to deliver well.
workshops.what: A one day diagnostic on your last three to five production briefs against a structured framework. Objective clarity, success criteria, budget framing, talent direction, technical requirements, decision rights.
workshops.outcome: A written Brief Diagnostic Report with brief by brief assessment, a redesigned brief template, and gate criteria for what counts as production ready.

workshops.name: Maturity Diagnostic
workshops.hook: Six dimensions. RAG scored. In a day.
workshops.why: You want a fast, structured picture of where your production function stands. Six dimensions, RAG scored, in a day. The faster cousin of the full Audit.
workshops.what: A one day diagnostic across six production dimensions (workflow, technology, budget, team, pipeline, vendors), scored alongside your team using SHIFT's diagnostic framework.
workshops.outcome: A written Maturity Diagnostic with RAG scores, top findings, and a recommended path forward, including whether a full Audit is warranted.

workshops.name: Insourcing Diagnostic
workshops.hook: A quantified test of whether insourcing makes sense for you.
workshops.why: You are considering bringing production in house but want a quantified test of whether it makes sense, at what scale, and what it would cost compared to your current agency model.
workshops.what: A one day diagnostic modelling cost, capability, and risk of insourcing scenarios. Compared against current agency managed costs. Scenario tested against likely production volumes.
workshops.outcome: A written Insourcing Diagnostic with scenario modelling, total cost of ownership comparison, and a recommendation on whether, when, and how to insource.

Practice.name: Practice Workshop
Practice.tagline: Master a discipline. Deep on one thing that matters.
Practice.description: A Practice Workshop runs over one or two days with the focused team in the room. The discipline is delivered by a senior advisor with deep, current experience, not generalist consulting theory. Every Practice Workshop is applied to real upcoming work the team is about to deliver, so what you learn lands the next week.
Practice.followup: Most Practice Workshops orbit a specific production where the discipline needs applying. After the Workshop, brands often invite SHIFT to lead that production with independent advisory.

workshops.name: Sustainable Production
workshops.hook: A structured approach to carbon targets that does not inflate costs.
workshops.why: Procurement, brand, and ESG teams are increasingly asking production to deliver against carbon and sustainability targets. You need a structured approach that does not inflate costs.
workshops.what: A one to two day session on carbon budgeting, sustainable supplier criteria, ESG reporting frameworks, and integrating sustainability into the production brief.
workshops.outcome: A Sustainable Production playbook covering supplier criteria, carbon budget templates, and reporting structures the team can apply on the next production.

workshops.name: Procurement-Production Alignment
workshops.hook: Both sides aligned. Without compromising production quality.
workshops.why: Procurement and production speak different languages. Decisions get blocked, costs get questioned late, trust erodes. You want both sides aligned without compromising production quality.
workshops.what: A one to two day session bridging procurement and production. Shared cost vocabulary, decision gates, escalation paths, supplier framework. Both functions in the room.
workshops.outcome: A joint Procurement-Production Operating Agreement with shared definitions, decision gates, and escalation paths the next production runs through.

workshops.name: Post-Production Cost Control
workshops.hook: Where budget overruns hide. Found and controlled.
workshops.why: Post production is where the budget overruns hide. Offline, online, grade, VFX, sound, music. You want a structured way to forecast, track, and control these costs.
workshops.what: A one day deep dive on post production cost structure. Benchmark rates by discipline, cost driver analysis, milestone gates, change order management, vendor terms.
workshops.outcome: A Post-Production Cost Control playbook covering budget structure, gate checklists, change order discipline, and benchmark rates the team applies immediately.

workshops.name: Music & Licensing Strategy
workshops.hook: Sync fees and territorial rights, handled properly.
workshops.why: Sync fees, buyout structures, and territorial rights are where serious money leaks and serious legal risk lives. Your team needs a defensible approach.
workshops.what: A one day specialist session on music rights, sync fee structures, buyout management, territorial rights, AI music governance, and licensing negotiation.
workshops.outcome: A Music & Licensing playbook covering rights frameworks, fee benchmarks, contract clauses, and a decision tree for licensed versus original music.

workshops.name: Cross-Border Production
workshops.hook: Cost, talent, and compliance across territories.
workshops.why: Producing across European or global territories adds cost, talent, and compliance complexity most brand teams are not equipped to manage.
workshops.what: A two day session on cross border production. Cost arbitrage, talent flow, tax credits, location selection, compliance regimes, vendor networks.
workshops.outcome: A Cross-Border Production playbook covering territory selection criteria, cost frameworks, compliance checklists, and a vendor network the team can deploy.

workshops.name: Social-First Production
workshops.hook: Production designed for the social-first reality.
workshops.why: Platform native, short form, AI augmented content does not work on the broadcast production model. You need a production approach designed for the social first reality.
workshops.what: A two day session on social first production design. Volume, format, talent, tools, AI integration, brand consistency at scale.
workshops.outcome: A Social-First Production playbook covering operating model, talent structure, tool stack, AI integration points, and a production rhythm the team executes against.

workshops.name: Live & Real-Time Production
workshops.hook: Capability rehearsed before the moment arrives.
workshops.why: Live events, breaking news content, and cultural moment production require a production capability most brands have not rehearsed. When the moment arrives, capability shows.
workshops.what: A one to two day session on live and real time production. Pre rehearsed workflows, on call talent networks, rapid approval protocols, technology stack, governance for in the moment decisions.
workshops.outcome: A Live & Real-Time Production playbook covering rehearsed workflows, talent network, approval protocols, and a capability the team can activate when needed.

Leadership.name: Leadership Workshop
Leadership.tagline: Work out your position together. Before the moment arrives.
Leadership.description: A Leadership Workshop is half a day to one day, senior audience only. The leadership team builds its position on a high stakes topic ahead of the conversation that needs it. The board, the CFO, the cultural flashpoint. SHIFT facilitates. The leadership team produces the answer. Every Leadership Workshop ends with a defensible position the room has built and signed off together.
Leadership.followup: Leaders who have had SHIFT in the room for a high stakes moment usually want us in the room for the next one. After a Leadership Workshop, the most common path is an ongoing advisory relationship with the leadership team.

workshops.name: The Board AI Workshop
workshops.hook: Walk into the board with a defensible AI position.
workshops.why: The board is going to ask the CMO what the brand's AI position is. The CMO needs to walk in with a defensible answer, not a deck of vendor logos.
workshops.what: A one day senior only working session that builds the brand's AI position. Strategic posture, governance commitments, investment priorities, risk framework. Backed by evidence the room produces together.
workshops.outcome: A written Board AI Position the leadership team has signed off, with the evidence and rationale that supports it.

workshops.name: Production ROI Workshop
workshops.hook: Production ROI in language finance can act on.
workshops.why: Finance and procurement are asking for production ROI in a language production teams cannot speak. You need a defensible answer that holds up in budget review.
workshops.what: A one day senior session defining production ROI in CFO friendly terms. Cost framework, value framework, measurement approach, reporting cadence.
workshops.outcome: A written Production ROI Framework with definitions, measurement plan, and reporting templates the team uses with finance from the next budget cycle.

workshops.name: AI Cost vs. Value Workshop
workshops.hook: What in your AI stack actually earns its place.
workshops.why: Your AI subscription stack is growing and you cannot tell what is genuinely saving cost or creating value versus what is being paid for and barely used.
workshops.what: A one day senior session modelling AI cost versus value across the production stack. Tool by tool analysis, usage data, outcome data. Decisions on rationalisation, scale up, and exit.
workshops.outcome: A written AI Cost vs. Value Picture with tool level decisions, rationalisation plan, and quarterly review cadence.
workshops.cta: Book your AI Cost vs. Value Workshop

workshops.name: Crisis Production Workshop
workshops.hook: A crisis production plan, rehearsed before the crisis.
workshops.why: The next cultural flashpoint, regulatory event, or brand crisis is coming. When it does, your production capability has to respond in hours, not weeks. Most brands have no rehearsed plan.
workshops.what: A one day senior working session designing the brand's crisis production playbook. Trigger criteria, decision rights, on call talent, pre rehearsed workflows, governance under pressure.
workshops.outcome: A written Crisis Production Playbook the leadership team has signed off and the production team can activate in hours.

Deliverables.title: What every SHIFT Workshop ends with.
Deliverables.intro: Every Workshop produces a written outcome the team owns. Not slides. A document.

items.label: Full documentation.
items.body: Everything that was discussed, decided, and left open, written up and shared with the team.

items.label: Full analysis.
items.body: A structured analysis of the inputs the team brought into the room. Briefs, budgets, tool inventories, rosters, production records. Every artefact gets a written read.

items.label: Next steps.
items.body: Concrete actions the team committed to in the room, with owners and timelines.

items.label: A recommendation.
items.body: Where it applies, a recommendation on the right SHIFT engagement to follow. Sometimes that is no follow up at all. Both are valid outcomes.

Closing.title: Talk to us about a Workshop.
Closing.body: Tell us where you are and what is coming up. We will scope, schedule, and run it with you.

Request.title: Book a Workshop
Request.subtitle: Tell us a bit about the situation. We will be back within 24 hours.
Request.success_body: We will be in touch within 24 hours, Monday to Friday.

Workshops.ProductSlideSubhead: After the Workshop, where it makes sense.

Meta.title: Pilot — shift.media
Meta.description: One project. Brief to delivery. SHIFT brings the senior advisory layer your team needs.

Hero.title: Pilot.
Hero.body: One project. Brief to delivery. Advisory leadership end to end.
Hero.subline: SHIFT on one real piece of work.

Definition.lead: A Pilot is full advisory leadership on one of your projects.
Definition.sub: Brief to delivery. Whatever the project needs.
Definition.p2: Some Pilots are oversight. Others go further. SHIFT designs a bespoke production workflow with your team for the duration of the project.
Definition.p3: Independent of the agency and the production company. The advice is only in your interest.

cards.title: Brand Campaign
cards.hook: A single integrated campaign across multiple formats and channels.
cards.detail: SHIFT leads production advisory across film, content, and social executions inside the campaign.

cards.title: Hero Film
cards.hook: A single high stakes hero film, TVC, or anthem piece.
cards.detail: SHIFT covers advisory across brief, director and production company selection, budget design, on set oversight, post discipline, and rights.

cards.title: Content Series
cards.hook: A defined content series or branded episodic content.
cards.detail: SHIFT designs the production model for the series, advises through the first episodes alongside the team, and hands over a repeatable framework.

cards.title: Brand Launch
cards.hook: The production work behind a brand launch, rebrand, or major positioning moment.
cards.detail: SHIFT covers the production strategy and the delivery rhythm across the launch window.

cards.title: AI Production
cards.hook: A first AI driven production at scale.
cards.detail: SHIFT covers tool selection, governance, output quality control, rights and disclosure, and the human in the loop standards that protect the brand.

cards.title: Live Event
cards.hook: A single live event production. Launch, summit, brand activation, festival.
cards.detail: SHIFT covers production strategy, vendor selection, rehearsed workflows, and live governance.

cards.title: Format Launch
cards.hook: Bringing a new format to life. Podcast, social series, mini documentary, branded short film.
cards.detail: SHIFT designs the production model for the format and advises through the first cycle.

phases.label: Scope
phases.description: We scope the Pilot with you. Project goals, success criteria, key constraints, SHIFT's role inside your team, vendor and agency context. We confirm what we cover, what stays with you, and what falls outside the Pilot.

phases.label: Embed
phases.description: SHIFT joins the project as advisory lead. Brief refinement, production company and director selection advisory, budget design, vendor negotiation support, governance setup. Minimum two days per week of active engagement, more if the project demands it.
phases.deliverable: Production plan, budget, partner selection, governance framework.

phases.label: Deliver
phases.description: The project goes into live production with SHIFT embedded in the rhythm as advisory lead. On set or in pre and post sessions, on calls, on approvals, on costs. Weekly Estimated versus Actual reporting. Real time decision support for the brand team.
phases.deliverable: Delivered project, weekly EvsA reports, decision log.

phases.label: Handover
phases.description: Post production close out, cost reconciliation, rights and disclosure check, lessons learned, recommendation for what comes next. SHIFT exits cleanly, with everything documented.
phases.deliverable: Final Pilot Report covering project delivery, cost reconciliation, lessons, and recommendation.

Scope.heading: In scope and out of scope.
in_items: Real time cost reporting and variance management
AI tool selection and governance design
out_items: Structural redesign of your full production operation — that is Build
Ongoing advisory across multiple workstreams beyond this project — that is Engine
Production lead on parallel campaigns running alongside this project — that is Campaign or an extended Pilot
Recruiting or hiring decisions for permanent staff — we advise, we do not place

items.label: The project, delivered.
items.body: Whatever you brought us in for. The film, the campaign, the series, the launch, the event, the rollout. Produced and shipped, with SHIFT as advisory lead alongside your team and your partners.

items.label: A full Pilot Report.
items.body: A written analysis of what was done, how it was built, what worked, what did not, the cost reconciliation against the original scope, and the lessons learned. Every artefact produced during the Pilot is included as appendix. The report belongs to your team.

items.label: A recommendation, where it makes sense.
items.body: Sometimes a Pilot surfaces something structural that would benefit from a longer engagement with SHIFT. Sometimes the project is the work, and the work is done. Both are valid outcomes.

Closing.title: Talk to us about a Pilot.
Closing.body: Tell us about the project. We will scope it, schedule it, and run it alongside your team.

Request.title: Start a Pilot conversation
Request.subtitle: Tell us about the project. We will get back to you within one working day.
Request.success_body: We will be in touch within one working day.

ProductSlide.heading: After the Pilot, where it makes sense.

cards.title: Campaign
cards.hook: Embedded production advisory on a specific campaign or production.
cards.modal_body_1: Campaign is end to end production advisory lead on a single defined production. A campaign, a film, a content series, a launch, an AI rollout, an event.
cards.modal_body_2: SHIFT sits alongside your team for the duration: joining calls, reviewing documents, providing real time input. Independent of the agency and the production company involved.
cards.modal_body_3: Typically four weeks to six months. Scoped fee. Ends when the campaign ships.

cards.title: Build
cards.hook: Advisory led design and build of your in house production capability.
cards.modal_body_1: Build is a multi month engagement to design and build the production function inside your brand. Operating model, org structure, workflow, technology, governance. Designed together, built, and handed over.
cards.modal_body_2: Designed for brands building production in house for the first time, restructuring an underperforming function, or moving production out of agency into in house.
cards.modal_body_3: Typically three to six months, phased. Fixed fee per phase. SHIFT exits cleanly when the function is operational.

cards.title: Engine
cards.hook: Ongoing senior advisory across content, campaigns, and operations.
cards.modal_body_1: Engine is a fixed monthly retainer covering an agreed scope of senior production advisory work. The senior advisor you would hire full time, without hiring full time.
cards.modal_body_2: Designed for brands with regular production volume or ongoing transformation, where consistent expert input is needed through the year. Engine spans the full production activity, not a single project.
cards.modal_body_3: Typically minimum six months. Monthly retainer. Quarterly review of scope and outcomes.
